---
title: "SBA 7(a) Loan Insights Dashboard"
author: "Esha Teware"
format: html
editor: visual
embed-resources: true
---

## 1. Admin

**App Title:** SBA 7(a) Loan Insights Dashboard\
**Course:** DATA 613 – Introduction to Data Science 004\
**Semester:** Fall 2025\
**Team Members:** Esha Teware

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. Topic and Data

### 2.1 Context of the Problem

Small businesses drive innovation and employment but often struggle to secure affordable credit. The U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) bridges this gap through its 7(a) loan program, providing government - guaranteed loans that reduce lender risk.\
This project uses the **FOIA SBA 7(a) Loan Dataset (FY 2020–Present)** to analyze national lending trends since the COVID-19 pandemic. The Shiny dashboard aims to visualize how SBA support has evolved over time and whether access to capital is distributed equitably across **states, industries, and demographics**.

The central question:\
\> *How have SBA 7(a) lending patterns, loan sizes, and outcomes varied across U.S. regions and borrower groups since 2020, and what factors are associated with loan default risk?*

The dashboard will help **policy analysts, researchers, and small-business advocates** explore lending equity, identify patterns in approval amounts, and evaluate indicators of loan performance.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 2.2 Literature Review

Research on SBA programs emphasizes their critical role in improving credit access but also notes persistent disparities.

1.  **Galli-Debicella (2020)** found SBA loans significantly increased small-firm survival rates (74% vs. 44%) but that loan size was not linked to success. This study focused on Connecticut firms in 1999.\
2.  **Lee et al. (2023)** examined regional redistribution effects of SBA-guaranteed loans and identified uneven lending across states and industries, shaped by banking capacity and local SBA offices.\
3.  **Craig, Rutherford, & Milliken (2019)** documented strong local employment growth in counties with higher SBA lending, reinforcing the program’s economic impact.

Building on this work, my project **extends** these studies to a modern, post-COVID context by analyzing the national FOIA dataset and exploring demographic and regional patterns using interactive visualization.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 2.3 Proposed Data Source

**Dataset:** FOIA – 7(a) (FY 2020–Present)\
**Link:** [data.sba.gov](https://data.sba.gov/en/dataset/7-a-504-foia/resource/d67d3ccb-2002-4134-a288-481b51cd3479)\
**Format:** CSV (\~153 MB)\
**Scope:** All SBA-guaranteed 7(a) loans approved nationwide since FY 2020 - 2024.

### Key Variables Used

| **Category** | **Variables** | **Purpose** |
|----|----|----|
| Borrower location | `BorrState`, `BorrCity` | Regional trends, mapping |
| Loan characteristics | `GrossApproval`, `TermInMonths`, `DeliveryMethod`, `CollateralInd` | Loan structure and size |
| Borrower characteristics | `BusinessType`, `BusinessAge`, `JobsSupported` | Business profile |
| Loan outcome | `LoanStatus`, `ChargeoffDate`, `GrossChargeoffAmount` | Default and repayment status |
| Time & program info | `AsOfDate`, `Program` | Time trends, program filtering |

**Data volume:** 330,705 observations, 43 variables.\
**Cleaning steps:** standardize column names, convert date and numeric formats, drop sensitive identifiers (`BorrName`, `BorrStreet`).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Use Case

### 3.1 Actor and Description

The primary **actor** is a **policy or economic development analyst** interested in evaluating how SBA lending supports small businesses nationwide.\
Secondary users include SBA staff, journalists, and students analyzing post-COVID small-business finance.

### 3.2 Actor Questions of Interest

1.  Which states and industries received the largest share of SBA loans since 2020?\
2.  How did lending change during the COVID-19 recovery years?\
3.  Do average loan sizes differ across borrower gender or race?\
4.  What factors are associated with loan charge-offs (defaults)?

### 3.3 Anticipated Workflow

1.  User selects filters for state, year range, industry, or demographics.\
2.  Dashboard dynamically updates visualizations.\
3.  User views model results showing which loan attributes predict default risk.\
4.  Insights are used for equity analysis and policy recommendations.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. Ethical Review

Although the FOIA dataset is public, it includes sensitive demographic data (`Gender`, `Race`). Ethical handling requires: - Aggregating and visualizing data at group or regional levels (no individual disclosure).\
- Avoiding misinterpretation of disparities by contextualizing results within systemic credit access issues.\
- Ensuring transparency of analysis steps and acknowledgment of data limitations (missing demographics, reporting bias).

The project aligns with the **ACM Code of Ethics** and promotes fairness, transparency, and responsible use of public financial data.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. App Concept

### 5.1 General Layout

The Shiny dashboard will include:

1\. **Overview Tab:** summary statistics and KPIs.\
2. **Regional Trends Tab:** choropleth map and bar charts of loan volumes by state.\
3. **Demographic Insights Tab:** comparisons of loan size and counts by gender and race.\
4. **Loan Outcomes Tab:** distribution of loan statuses and logistic model results.\
5. **About & Ethics Tab:** data source, limitations, and ethical statement.

### 5.2 User Data Selection

Interactive sidebar filters for: - Year range (2020–2025)\
- State\
- Industry (NAICS 2-digit)\
- Business age (startup vs. existing)\
- Gender and race

### 5.3 Exploratory Analysis

-   Summary statistics for loan count, total amount, and average approval.\
-   Bar and line charts for industry and time trends.\
-   Interactive map visualizing regional totals and averages.

### 5.4 Statistical Model

To explore factors affecting loan default:

```{r,eval=FALSE}
# Logistic regression predicting loan default 
glm(Default ~ GrossApproval + TermInMonths + BusinessAge + CollateralInd + DeliveryMethod + BorrState, family = "binomial")
```

## 6. Collaboration

### 6.1 Roles and Responsibilities

This is an **individual project** completed solely by *Esha Teware*.\
I am responsible for all aspects of the project, including: - Data cleaning and preparation of the FOIA 7(a) Loan dataset\
- Exploratory data analysis and visualization design\
- Statistical modeling and interpretation\
- Development of the Shiny dashboard user interface\
- Documentation, ethics statement, and final report formatting

### 6.2 Workflow and Branching

Although working independently, I will follow a structured workflow similar to a collaborative environment:

1\. Maintain a GitHub repository with branches for `data-clean`, `eda`, `model`, and `app`.\
2. Commit changes frequently with clear messages.\
3. Merge tested code into the `main` branch after checkpoints.\
4. Track milestones and code updates through Git version control.

### 6.3 Schedule and Milestones

| Phase  | Deliverable                             | Target Date |
|--------|-----------------------------------------|-------------|
| Week 2 | Data cleaning & initial EDA             | Oct 31      |
| Week 4 | Visualization and layout draft          | Nov 10      |
| Week 6 | Shiny app prototype ready               | Nov 20      |
| Week 7 | Logistic model integration & testing    | Nov 26      |
| Week 8 | Final vignette (.qmd + HTML) submission | Dec 4       |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 7. Readability

The final document will use clear Quarto formatting with embedded R code and reproducible outputs.\
All figures will include descriptive titles, captions, and legends.\
Spelling, grammar, and APA-style citations will be reviewed before submission to ensure professional presentation and compliance with course readability standards.
